name: Oud Al Layali
description: A deep, resinous blend of aged agarwood and warm amber. Burns long and slowly, leaving a trail of dark sweetness that lingers through the night. This is bakhoor for the ceremony of rest — light it when the day is done.
how_to_use: Place one to two pieces on a burning charcoal disc or electric bakhoor burner. Allow 60–90 seconds for the smoke to develop before moving the burner through your space. One piece perfumes a room for 20–30 minutes.

name: Zahrat Al Sabah
description: A lighter, floral bakhoor for the morning ritual. Rose water and white oud open the senses gently, like sunlight through silk curtains. The smoke is soft and dissipates cleanly — made for quiet mornings, not grand ceremony.
how_to_use: Use one piece on low heat to keep the smoke light and transparent. Ideal for smaller rooms, bathrooms, or as a personal scent ritual before dressing. Pairs well with morning coffee.

name: Ramad
description: Our most modern blend. Smoky, dry, with an edge of burnt wood and vetiver. For those who wear their home like a signature — Ramad is deliberate, architectural, unforgettable. A bakhoor that demands a room worthy of it.
how_to_use: Use sparingly — one piece is enough for a large living room. Best on traditional charcoal for maximum smoke and intensity. Allow 10 minutes to fully develop before guests arrive.

name: Nour
description: A luminous, transparent blend. Citrus woods and light resins create a clean, radiant smoke — effortless for daily use in any European interior. Nour bridges traditions: familiar enough for those new to bakhoor, refined enough for connoisseurs.
how_to_use: The most versatile RAHA blend. Use one piece on an electric burner for a gentle, continuous scent or on charcoal for a stronger presence. Ideal as an everyday home fragrance.
